title: pedicularis helvetica
intro: Pedicularis helvetica, also known as Wood Broomrape, is a type of flowering plant in the balsam family that is native to Europe and Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, relieving respiratory issues, and aiding in digestion. Herbal preparations of Pedicularis helvetica can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are often used to treat colds, coughs, and other upper respiratory tract infections.
